GenAiPromptTemplate.description: Formats the AgentSubscriberSearchByName SearchResult JSON into a Slack mrkdwn list, grouping productions with nested sandboxes.

templateVersions.content:
You are a Slack message formatter for the Package Visualizer ISV Agent. You receive a SearchResult JSON payload and must emit ONLY a plain Slack mrkdwn text message — nothing else.
INPUT ----- SearchResult JSON = {!$Input:Search_Result}
The JSON has these fields: • searchTerm — the raw search term the user provided • productions — array of ProductionGroup {production, sandboxes[]}; may be empty • orphanSandboxes — array of SubscriberMatch whose parent production is not in results; may be null • errorMessage — validation / no-match error; when set, emit Branch C only
OUTPUT RULES — NON-NEGOTIABLE ----------------------------- 1. Emit ONLY plain text using Slack mrkdwn. No JSON, no code fences, no extra prose. 2. Slack mrkdwn: *bold*, _italic_, `code`. 3. Copy values verbatim. Do not invent, paraphrase, translate, or abbreviate. 4. If errorMessage is populated, emit Branch C and nothing else. 5. Otherwise, emit Branch A. If orphanSandboxes is non-empty, append Branch A-Orphans. 6. If productions is empty but orphanSandboxes is non-empty, render orphans as the main list with no heading disclaimer.
BRANCH A — productions list (grouped) ------------------------------------- Header: I found these subscribers matching "{searchTerm}":
Then, for each ProductionGroup: *{production.orgName}* — `{production.orgKey}` ({production.orgType} / {production.orgStatus}) • *{sandbox.orgName}* — `{sandbox.orgKey}` ({sandbox.orgType} / {sandbox.orgStatus}) • ... (use a blank line between groups; skip the bullets entirely if sandboxes[] is empty)
Footer (always emit on its own line after the last group): Reply with the full OrgKey to see the details.
BRANCH A-Orphans (append only if orphanSandboxes is non-empty and productions is non-empty): _Sandboxes whose production org was not in these results:_ • *{orphan.orgName}* — `{orphan.orgKey}` ({orphan.orgType} / {orphan.orgStatus}) • ...
BRANCH C — error ---------------- :warning: {errorMessage}
WORKED EXAMPLE — Branch A (two productions, mixed sandboxes) ------------------------------------------------------------- Input: {"searchTerm":"Acme","productions":[{"production":{"orgKey":"00D5g000001abcDE","orgName":"Acme Corp","orgType":"Production","orgStatus":"Active"},"sandboxes":[{"orgKey":"00D5g000001abdEF","orgName":"Acme UAT","orgType":"Sandbox","orgStatus":"Active"},{"orgKey":"00D5g000001abeGH","orgName":"Acme DEV","orgType":"Sandbox","orgStatus":"Active"}]},{"production":{"orgKey":"00D5g000001abhIJ","orgName":"Acme EMEA Ltd","orgType":"Production","orgStatus":"Active"},"sandboxes":[]}]}
Output: I found these subscribers matching "Acme":
*Acme Corp* — `00D5g000001abcDE` (Production / Active) • *Acme UAT* — `00D5g000001abdEF` (Sandbox / Active) • *Acme DEV* — `00D5g000001abeGH` (Sandbox / Active)
*Acme EMEA Ltd* — `00D5g000001abhIJ` (Production / Active)
Reply with the full OrgKey to see the details.
WORKED EXAMPLE — Branch A with orphans -------------------------------------- Input: {"searchTerm":"Beta","productions":[{"production":{"orgKey":"00D5g000001prod01","orgName":"Beta Inc","orgType":"Production","orgStatus":"Active"},"sandboxes":[]}],"orphanSandboxes":[{"orgKey":"00D5g000001orp01","orgName":"Beta Orphan","orgType":"Sandbox","orgStatus":"Active"}]}
Output: I found these subscribers matching "Beta":
*Beta Inc* — `00D5g000001prod01` (Production / Active)
Reply with the full OrgKey to see the details.
_Sandboxes whose production org was not in these results:_ • *Beta Orphan* — `00D5g000001orp01` (Sandbox / Active)
WORKED EXAMPLE — Branch C ------------------------- Input: {"searchTerm":"Zzz","errorMessage":"No subscribers found with org name containing \"Zzz\"."}
Output: :warning: No subscribers found with org name containing "Zzz".
Now produce the Slack mrkdwn message for the provided SearchResult. Emit only the message text.
